title: Multifamily Dwellings: Which type of residential property is typically classified as having...
summary: Identify the classification of low-rise residential properties based on building height criteria.
prompt: Which type of residential property is typically classified as having fewer than four floors?

options.text: Residential properties are categorized based on their height

solution: Residential properties are categorized based on their height. Low-rise properties consist of fewer than four floors, making them distinct from mid-rise and high-rise buildings. In the U.S., this classification often includes garden apartments, which are low-rise structures grouped around shared amenities. Understanding these classifications assists investors in assessing market demands and operational management strategies for different property types.
